pending.body: finding_summary: Resuming a terminal `iterative_minimal` stage with a missing parsed sidecar re-runs the last implementer iteration on top of the already-mutated workspace instead of restoring the pre-iteration tree. In `resume_terminal_iterative_stage_result` the code rewinds only the counters (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6494-6545`), and `execute_iterative_plan_and_implement_stage` then immediately re-invokes iteration `N` after just persisting that rewound state (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6926-6945`). If the terminal exit reason was `max_rounds`, this effectively gives the implementer an extra pass beyond the configured cap and can apply additional edits on a different input tree than the original final iteration. affected_files: ["src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs"] remediation_hint: Either recover the terminal result from durable artifacts/raw output, or restore the worktree to the pre-iteration state before re-invoking; otherwise fail the run instead of replaying on top of the post-iteration workspace.

finding_summary: The git-based diff fingerprint misses empty-directory creations and removals, so an `iterative_minimal` round can be counted as stable even when the workspace changed. `git_diff_fingerprint` only hashes tracked diffs plus paths returned by `git ls-files --others` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6797-6852`), but Git does not report empty directories there. Creating or deleting an empty directory in a git repo therefore leaves `diff_before == diff_after`, increments `stable_count`, and can terminate the loop early. affected_files: ["src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs"] remediation_hint: Supplement the git-based fingerprint with a filesystem directory walk, or otherwise ensure empty directories participate in the fingerprint when git is available.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 13 -> Plan and Implement
